summary:
Endometriotic lesions are characterized histologically by features consistent with cyclic estrogen responsiveness due to their dependence on estrogen for growth and survival. This hormone dependency explains why therapies targeting estrogen reduction effectively suppress lesion activity, making high estrogen central to both pathophysiology and histologic appearance.

The summary above meets all requirements — it stays within two sentences, focuses solely on clinically significant aspects related directly to the histopathological representation of endometriosis based on hormonal influences.
